summaries.90805: Comments confirm the bug affects web and desktop platforms but not mobile. On master at the time, selection values were correct numerically but the visible highlight was still dropped on button click. A team member identified this as standard browser behavior where clicking a button removes focus from the input element, and reassigned the issue to the framework team to investigate preserving selection on button click.
summaries.66356: Root cause identified in comments: the web engine creates the native DOM input element dynamically and calls focus() programmatically, but browsers only show the autofill dropdown when the user physically clicks on the element — not when focus is set via script. Confirmed still reproducing through Flutter 3.19/3.21. A workaround noted is setting autofocus: true, which causes the field to show suggestions on first click. Another workaround is using Platform Views for a login form. Issue stalled with no fix.
summaries.105725: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on Android and all platforms during animation when widthFactor is between 0 and 1. Suspected root cause is anti-aliasing at clipped boundaries. Team deferred investigation, suggesting the user ask on Stack Overflow. No fix or PR proposed.
summaries.40582: Comments confirm the bug still reproduces through Flutter 3.6 on stable and master. Root cause is that a setState() call that changes the widget tree while the keyboard is open causes a rebuild that closes the keyboard, specifically when widget types change (e.g., Spacer replaced by Container). No fix proposed; issue remains stalled.
summaries.67282: Comments confirm the root cause: iOS's UIKeyboardType.namePhonePad (used by TextInputType.name) explicitly does not support auto-capitalization, so textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.words is ignored. Issue is iOS-only; Android works correctly. Three workarounds documented: (1) avoid TextInputType.name, (2) use an InputFormatter to force capitalization, (3) file a PR. Confirmed still reproducing through stable 3.29 and master 3.32 on iOS 18.4.
summaries.25972: Comments confirm the bug affects Android. Root cause is that after a TextField loses focus and the keyboard is dismissed, subsequent map touch gestures (pinch/move) are not properly handled. A workaround provided by a commenter: add gestureRecognizers: Set()..add(Factory<EagerGestureRecognizer>(() => EagerGestureRecognizer())) to the GoogleMap widget. Issue otherwise stalled.
summaries.78421: Root cause partially identified: the Linux engine emits a warning message 'Event response for event id ... received, but pending event was not found' whenever keyboard input stops working. Reporter suspects it correlates with machines that have slower processor speeds (1.2–1.3 GHz). The issue is intermittent, harder to reproduce on VMs, and a separate commenter reports the bug also occurs with the space key causing inkwell effects instead of inserting text. No fix or PR proposed; stalled.
summaries.17704: Comments identify the root cause: FormField's validator() callbacks are invoked during the build phase (inside FormFieldState.build), so calling setState() inside a validator triggers the 'setState() called during build' assertion. A team discussion noted a workaround using scheduleMicrotask() and another using Form's onChanged. Long-term, the team agreed Form should not call validators during build. A commenter identified the specific line in form.dart responsible. Issue remains open with no fix.
summaries.149480: Comments confirm the Control modifier does not work with CharacterActivator on Windows (both stable 3.22 and master 3.23) and macOS/Android/iOS, while Alt works alone. Linux desktop works correctly. A related issue is #99255. A team member suggested Ctrl+G generates a 'Bell' ASCII character, but the reporter confirmed it fails with all alphabet/number keys, not just Ctrl+G. No fix or PR proposed; stalled.
summaries.80123: Root cause identified: physical keyboard input in UITextView platform views fails because Flutter 2.0 introduced a null check error in ShortcutManager.handleKeypress when no focus is set. PR #80302 was created to be more tolerant of apps with no focused widget. Confirmed fixed for the original reporter in Flutter 2.5.3. The issue also affected WKWebView and webview_flutter. PR #80302 landed.
summaries.135812: Comments narrow down the issue to iOS 17 specifically (one tester with iOS 16.6 could not reproduce). The 3-finger tap triggering the iOS copy/paste bar after TextField destruction also occurs when a 3-finger swipe triggers undo/redo and the user navigates away. Issue was assigned to @Renzo-Olivares but the assignee was removed after extended inactivity. No fix or workaround found.
summaries.145524: Comments confirm the bug: a Flutter web app embedded in an HTML page's modal/dialog via iframe cannot focus or edit TextFields. Confirmed reproducing on Flutter 3.19.4 and 3.21. A live demo was provided at kaonick.github.io. The issue is that the browser's focus/input handling is disrupted by the cross-frame iframe context. No fix or PR proposed.
summaries.111084: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on Flutter 3.3/3.4 stable and master. The issue is that TapGestureRecognizer on TextSpans that are scrolled out of the initial visible area of a TextField return incorrect hit positions — tapping out-of-view spans fires callbacks for spans that were originally visible. Investigation was deferred to @justinmc, @LongCatIsLooong, and @Renzo-Olivares with no fix proposed.
summaries.135800: Comments identify two distinct cases: (1) keyboard opens but FAB jumps to top of screen and TextField has wrong insets, similar to #56039; (2) keyboard opens but TextField disappears, related to #124205. Confirmed reproducing on stable 3.13.6 and master 3.16. A commenter suggested setting resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false for Android web and applying iOS-specific unfocus handling. An additional report shows the FAB jumping on Android when dismissing a bottom sheet after keyboard use. Issue stalled.
summaries.138190: Root cause identified in comments: PR #136291 changed onTapOutside to only fire when the TextField has focus, so by the time the pointer-up event arrives, focus is already lost and the callback is not called. The design decision was intentional but users argue it prevents distinguishing taps from drags. Team discussed reverting #136291 or adding onTapUpOutside/onTapDownOutside callbacks. PR #156110 was referenced as a potential fix; no confirmation it resolved the issue.
summaries.94249: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable 2.5.3 and master 2.6. The keyboard briefly appears and immediately disappears when navigating forward in an inner Navigator while a TextField in the previous route had focus. Routed to @LongCatIsLooong and @chunhtai. No fix or PR proposed; stalled.
summaries.102239: Comments confirm the flashing composing underline only occurs with a hardware (physical) keyboard, not the software keyboard, on Android. Reproduced on both emulator (Pixel 4, API 31) and physical devices (Redmi K20 Pro). The physical device reproduction was confirmed on both stable 2.10.5 and master. No root cause or fix proposed; issue stalled.
summaries.59707: Comments clarify that the context menu IS present but scrolled out of view — it appears at the top of the selected text, which is above the viewport after 'Select All' scrolls to the bottom. On master at one point the menu moved below the selection, but it then gets obscured by the software keyboard. Confirmed reproducing on stable 3.3.8 and master 3.6 on both Android and iOS. Identified as likely a duplicate of #56176. No fix proposed.
summaries.105756: Root cause identified: the selection highlight is computed from TextBox rectangles that only cover the actual text width, not the full line width. A commenter investigated using BoxWidthStyle and LineMetrics but found that LineMetrics.width does not include trailing whitespace, making a complete solution require changes to paragraph.cc in the engine. A proof-of-concept code snippet was posted but noted as imperfect. No PR proposed; issue stalled.
summaries.98377: Root cause identified: on Windows, HardwareKeyboard.lockModesEnabled reports the inverted Caps Lock state when the app starts with Caps Lock already enabled because the engine does not query the initial lock state from the OS on startup. The issue also affects Linux. On macOS, the lock mode isn't added to lockModesEnabled until another key event fires. A related issue #98533 is linked. Comments confirm the bug persists through Flutter 3.19 and that lockModesEnabled also fails to update when Caps Lock is toggled while the app window is not focused. No fix or PR proposed.
